## PC6: RULES (Constraints & Guardrails)

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: explicit_mode_boundaries

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-005, KA-006, KA-019, KA-021

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: explicit_mode_boundaries
constraint: |
  All agent operations MUST declare an explicit operational mode at session start.
  Mode switches require explicit declaration and context reloading.
  Implicit mode switching is prohibited.

rationale: |
  Prevents task drift - explicit mode boundaries reduce drift by 34% compared to 
  implicit switching per KA-006. Also prevents "God Agent" anti-pattern (KA-019) 
  where single agent handles all tasks, leading to context overflow and poor 
  specialization. Temperature must match mode (KA-021).

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Monitor for mode declaration in first 100 tokens of session.
    Flag sessions without explicit mode declaration.
    Track tool usage patterns inconsistent with declared mode.
  response: |
    If mode not declared: Prompt agent to declare mode before proceeding.
    If mode mismatch detected: Request mode switch with context reload.
    If God Agent pattern detected: Force decomposition into specialized modes.
  severity: high

exceptions:
  - condition: Emergency recovery scenarios requiring rapid mode switching
    requires: Post-hoc mode declaration and drift assessment
  - condition: Automated orchestration with predefined mode sequence
    requires: Mode sequence declared in workflow configuration

atoms: [KA-005, KA-006, KA-019, KA-021]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: temperature_by_task_type

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-021

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: temperature_by_task_type
constraint: |
  Temperature settings MUST match task type:
  - Code generation: 0.1 (consistency required)
  - Code review: 0.1 (consistency required)
  - Documentation: 0.3 (some creativity acceptable)
  - Brainstorming: 0.7 (high creativity desired)
  - Factual Q&A: 0.0 (deterministic)
  Using wrong temperature causes inconsistent generation or hallucinated facts.

rationale: |
  Prevents hallucination and inconsistency. Code generation and review require
  maximum consistency (0.1). Documentation benefits from slight creativity (0.3).
  Brainstorming requires high creativity (0.7). Factual answers must be
  deterministic (0.0).

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Check temperature setting against declared task type.
    Monitor output consistency for unexpected variance.
    Flag hallucinations in factual contexts.
  response: |
    If temperature mismatch: Adjust temperature to match task type.
    If hallucination detected: Reduce temperature and regenerate.
    If inconsistent output: Flag for review and adjust parameters.
  severity: critical

exceptions:
  - condition: Explicit experimentation with temperature effects
    requires: Documented experiment with controlled conditions
  - condition: Creative coding tasks (prototypes, exploration)
    requires: Task tagged as exploratory, not production

atoms: [KA-021]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: context_poisoning_protection

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-030, KA-031, KA-085, KA-086

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: context_poisoning_protection
constraint: |
  Once a session's context is compromised by poisoning, the ENTIRE session MUST be 
  treated as disposable. No reliable recovery exists other than starting fresh.
  Sessions have integrity boundaries; crossing them invalidates entire session.
  
  Attack vectors to monitor (KA-085):
  - Model Hallucination (internal, hidden)
  - Code Comments (external, visible)
  - Contaminated Input (user, often hidden)
  - Context Overflow (architectural, hidden)

rationale: |
  Context Poisoning (KA-030): Malicious or low-quality context corrupts agent 
  reasoning. "Wake-up prompts" don't work (KA-086) - poisoned context persists
  in conversational buffer. Hard session reset required.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Anomaly detection on context embeddings (KA-030)
    Consistency checking across retrieved documents
    Provenance tracking for context sources
    Pattern matching for known attack vectors (KA-085)
  response: |
    If poisoning suspected: 
      1. Halt current operation
      2. Document suspicious context
      3. TERMINATE session (KA-031 - disposable session principle)
      4. Start fresh session with sanitized context
    If wake-up prompt attempted: Reject and force session restart (KA-086)
  severity: critical

exceptions:
  - condition: Low-confidence suspicion with no sensitive operations pending
    requires: Enhanced monitoring with anomaly detection active
  - condition: Controlled security testing
    requires: Isolated test environment with no production access

atoms: [KA-030, KA-031, KA-085, KA-086]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: complexity_budget_enforcement

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-053, KA-005

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: complexity_budget_enforcement
constraint: |
  All generated code MUST adhere to allocated complexity budgets.
  AI-generated code has 30% more abstraction layers and 20% more verbosity
  than human-written code. Explicit complexity limits reduce this tendency.
  Violation of "Vibe Coding" anti-pattern (KA-005) if unchecked.

rationale: |
  Complexity budgets reduce defect density by 40% when enforced consistently (KA-053).
  Without constraints, agents generate over-abstracted, verbose code.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Measure cyclomatic complexity per function
    Count abstraction layers (inheritance, composition depth)
    Measure verbosity ratio (tokens per logical operation)
    Compare against budget thresholds
  response: |
    If complexity exceeds budget: Reject code, request simplification
    If abstraction layers excessive: Require flattening
    If verbosity high: Request concise rewrite
    Track violations for agent calibration
  severity: medium

exceptions:
  - condition: Explicit architectural complexity requirement
    requires: Documented justification and approved exception
  - condition: Legacy code maintenance preserving existing complexity
    requires: Gradual refactoring plan with intermediate targets

atoms: [KA-053, KA-005]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: test_pyramid_compliance

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-057, KA-064, KA-065, KA-066

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: test_pyramid_compliance
constraint: |
  Generated test suites MUST follow pyramid proportions:
  - ~70% unit tests
  - ~20% integration tests  
  - ~10% end-to-end tests
  Inverted pyramid (more E2E than unit) is PROHIBITED per KA-065.
  Sad path testing is REQUIRED - 60-70% of production failures come from
  untested error paths per KA-057. AI-generated tests focus 80% on happy paths
  without explicit instruction per KA-066.

rationale: |
  Test Inversion Anti-Pattern (KA-065): More E2E than unit tests creates
  long feedback cycles, high maintenance cost, flaky tests, difficulty
  isolating failures. Sad path testing prevents 60-70% of production failures.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Count test types and calculate proportions
    Identify happy path bias in test coverage
    Check for error path testing
    Validate 80% line coverage minimum per KA-064
  response: |
    If pyramid inverted: Reject test suite, require rebalancing
    If sad paths missing: Explicitly request error path tests
    If coverage <80%: Require additional tests
    If happy path bias detected: Apply sad path prompt template
  severity: high

exceptions:
  - condition: System-level integration testing focus (e.g., API gateway)
    requires: Documented rationale and approval
  - condition: Legacy system with existing inverted pyramid
    requires: Gradual refactoring plan with incremental targets

atoms: [KA-057, KA-064, KA-065, KA-066]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: deadlock_prevention

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-013, KA-015, KA-083

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: deadlock_prevention
constraint: |
  Multi-agent workflows MUST implement deadlock prevention.
  Deadlock occurs when agents wait indefinitely for resources held by each other.
  Rates: 2-7% in complex workflows without explicit prevention per KA-015.
  
  Required mechanisms:
  - Resource ordering (acquire resources in defined order)
  - Time limits on resource acquisition
  - 3f+1 agents for Byzantine fault tolerance per KA-083

rationale: |
  Deadlock prevention critical for multi-agent systems. Adaptive throttling
  maintains 95th percentile latency within 2x baseline under 5x load (KA-013).
  Without prevention, workflows hang indefinitely.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Monitor wait-for graphs for cycles
    Track resource acquisition timeouts
    Detect agents waiting beyond time limits
    Flag Byzantine fault scenarios
  response: |
    If deadlock detected:
      1. Terminate involved agents
      2. Preempt resources
      3. Rollback to consistent state
      4. Restart with prevention mechanisms
    If timeout approaching: Alert and prepare recovery
    If Byzantine fault suspected: Isolate and use 3f+1 consensus
  severity: critical

exceptions:
  - condition: Single-agent workflows with no resource sharing
    requires: None - rule does not apply
  - condition: Explicitly designed wait states (e.g., human approval)
    requires: Documented timeout and escalation path

atoms: [KA-013, KA-015, KA-083]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

## PC7: CONTEXT MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES

---

PRODUCT: PC7 - CONTEXT STRATEGIES  
INSTANCE: budget_aware_retrieval

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-011, KA-032, KA-033, KA-034, KA-035, KA-036, KA-043, KA-046, KA-047, KA-048, KA-049, KA-087

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
strategy: budget_aware_retrieval
applies_to: 
  - Large codebase navigation
  - Multi-file feature implementation
  - Complex debugging scenarios
  - Long-running tasks

window_partition:
  system_instructions: 
    percentage: 10
    placement: top
    content: Mode definition, critical constraints, safety rules
  task_context:
    percentage: 20
    placement: after system
    content: Current task description, acceptance criteria
  relevant_code:
    percentage: 50
    placement: middle (U-shaped placement per KA-032)
    content: Retrieved code with relevance scoring
  history:
    percentage: 15
    placement: end
    content: Recent operations and decisions
  reserved_for_output:
    percentage: 5
    placement: end
    content: Space for response generation

content_selection:
  include:
    - Entrypoint-identified code per KA-048 (60-80% scope reduction)
    - High-relevance files per KA-049 (50-70% time reduction)
    - Semantic search results per KA-046, KA-047
    - RAG for Code results per KA-087 (67% hallucination reduction)
  exclude:
    - Files below relevance threshold
    - Duplicate or redundant context
    - Outdated cached information
    - Known poisoned sources

compression_pipeline:
  1: 
    technique: Semantic caching using embeddings
    threshold: Similarity >0.85
    benefit: 31-90% input token reduction per KA-011
  2:
    technique: LLMLingua prompt compression
    threshold: 20x compression with <3% degradation per KA-033
  3:
    technique: Hierarchical summarization
    threshold: Multi-level zoom per KA-037
    fallback: Progressive Disclosure (KA-008) - Level 1/2/3

ordering_rules:
  - Place critical constraints at boundaries (U-shaped per KA-032)
  - Place most relevant code near end for output generation
  - Group related context together
  - Separate system from task from code

refresh_policy: |
  Rotate context when:
  - Task phase changes (per workflow phases)
  - Relevance score drops below threshold
  - Token budget exceeded
  - Context poisoning suspected (KA-030)

poisoning_checks:
  - Anomaly detection on context embeddings (KA-030)
  - Consistency checking across sources
  - Provenance verification
  - Known attack vector scanning (KA-085)

atoms: [KA-011, KA-032, KA-033, KA-034, KA-035, KA-036, KA-043, KA-046, KA-047, KA-048, KA-049, KA-087]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

GAPS:
  - No validated token budget allocation algorithm across task phases
  - No standardized context refresh schedules for long tasks
  - Limited research on optimal compression thresholds

```yaml
pattern: hierarchical_decomposition_with_dag
purpose: Decompose complex tasks into executable DAG with optimal depth

depth_guidelines:
  simple_tasks: 2-3 levels (KA-023)
  complex_sdlc_workflows: 5-7 levels (KA-023)
  over_decomposition_warning: Increases coordination overhead
  under_decomposition_warning: Creates unmanageable units

procedure:
  1: Analyze task complexity and identify natural boundaries
  2: Decompose hierarchically (2-7 levels based on complexity) per KA-023
  3: Identify dependencies between decomposed units
  4: Convert to DAG representation per KA-029
  5: Apply fair-share scheduling per KA-014 (89% starvation reduction)
  6: Execute asynchronously for 2.3x speedup per KA-027

combination_recipe: |
  Hierarchical Task Decomposition + DAG-Based Execution + Worktree Isolation (KA-029):
  1. Decompose into task tree 2-7 levels deep per KA-023
  2. Convert to DAG with explicit dependencies
  3. Execute in isolated worktrees per KA-024 (67% conflict reduction)
  4. Merge with semantic resolution per KA-025

parallelization_strategy:
  approach: Async DAG execution (KA-027)
  speedup: 2.3x over sequential for typical SDLC workflows
  scheduling: Fair-share to prevent starvation (KA-014)
  isolation: Worktree per task to prevent conflicts (KA-024)

byzantine_considerations:
  agent_count: 3f+1 for f Byzantine failures (KA-083)
  application: Use for critical task validation
  overhead: Additional agents for consensus

mixture_of_agents:
  use_when: Quality-critical tasks
  benefit: 8-12% improvement over single-agent (KA-017)
  cost: 3-5x compute overhead (KA-017)

atoms: [KA-014, KA-017, KA-019, KA-023, KA-027, KA-029]

  2. Convert to DAG with dependencies
  3. Create worktree per task
  4. Execute in isolation

## PC10: TECHNIQUES & STRATEGIES (Situational Playbook)

---

PRODUCT: PC10 - TECHNIQUES & STRATEGIES  
INSTANCE: stale_spec_recovery

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-003, KA-004, KA-009, KA-050

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
strategy: stale_spec_recovery
purpose: Detect and recover from specification divergence

detection:
  method: Specification-code diff analysis
  trigger: Agent execution against outdated assumptions
  indicator: Implementation contradicts specification
  metric: Track spec-code consistency over time

failure_mode: |
  Stale Documentation Specs (KA-009): Human-only specification maintenance diverges 
  from implementation. Critique of spec-driven approaches: Over-specification leads 
  to "spec rot" where documentation diverges from implementation (KA-050).

response:
  1: Detect divergence through diff analysis
  2: Identify which is correct - spec or implementation
  3: Update incorrect side bidirectionally (KA-004)
  4: Document decision rationale
  5: Schedule regular spec-code sync reviews

prevention:
  technique: Bidirectional specification maintenance (KA-004)
  implementation: Agents update specs alongside code changes
  benefit: Prevents specification debt accumulation
  
tradeoff_consideration: |
  Spec-Driven vs Intent-Driven (KA-003):
  - Spec-driven: High reproducibility but high maintenance
  - Intent-driven: Lower maintenance but variable reproducibility
  - Decision: Use spec-driven for regulated/safety-critical, intent-driven for exploratory

atoms: [KA-003, KA-004, KA-009, KA-050]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC10 - TECHNIQUES & STRATEGIES  
INSTANCE: cost_optimization_playbook

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-010, KA-011, KA-012, KA-033, KA-036

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
strategy: cost_optimization_playbook
purpose: Minimize token costs while maintaining quality

cost_drivers: |
  Unconstrained agents burn $5-8 per task (KA-010):
  - Multi-turn reasoning: 40-60%
  - Tool calls: 20-30%
  - Context accumulation: 15-25%
  - Verification loops: 10-20%
  Output:input token ratio: 4-8:1 drives compression needs

optimization_techniques:
  1: Semantic Caching (KA-011)
     benefit: 31-90% input token reduction
     implementation: Store embedding of query, retrieve on similarity threshold
  
  2: Model Cascade Routing (KA-012)
     benefit: 60-87% cost reduction
     implementation: Start with cheap models, escalate only when needed
     example: EvoRoute - 76% cost reduction, 14% latency improvement
  
  3: LLMLingua Compression (KA-033)
     benefit: 20x compression with <3% performance degradation
     implementation: Prompt compression algorithms
  
  4: Avoid Context Stuffing (KA-036)
     warning: Naive filling wastes 23-45% tokens
     solution: Budget-aware retrieval with relevance scoring (KA-034)

anti_pattern: |
  Context Stuffing (KA-036): Maximally filling context windows without prioritization
  Failure modes: 23-45% tokens wasted, "lost in the middle" buries critical info
  Mitigation: Relevance-based filtering, budget-aware retrieval, U-shaped placement

implementation_priority:
  1: Semantic caching (highest impact, easiest)
  2: Model cascade routing (high impact, moderate complexity)
  3: Compression techniques (moderate impact, easy)
  4: Budget-aware retrieval (prevents waste)

atoms: [KA-010, KA-011, KA-012, KA-033, KA-036]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

### Highest Confidence Specs

1. **Mode: Planner** (KA-002, KA-006, KA-076, KA-078) - Strong evidence for BDI, mode boundaries, autonomy levels
2. **Rule: Context Poisoning Protection** (KA-030, KA-031, KA-085, KA-086) - Clear constraints with enforcement
3. **Skill: Code Traversal** (KA-046, KA-047, KA-048, KA-049) - Multiple high-evidence techniques
4. **Workflow: Spec-Driven Development** (KA-001, KA-004, KA-007) - Strong quantitative metrics
5. **Context Strategy: Budget-Aware** (KA-011, KA-032, KA-033, KA-034) - Comprehensive research backing

### Key Gaps Requiring Research

1. **Mode Transition Protocols**: No validated handoff procedures between modes
2. **Confidence Calibration**: No threshold validation for escalation decisions
3. **Agent Weighting**: No algorithm for aggregating multi-agent results
4. **Compression Thresholds**: Limited guidance on when to apply compression techniques
5. **Spec-Code Diff**: No validated tool for detecting specification drift
6. **Notification Fatigue**: No research on optimal batching strategies
7. **Pattern Extraction**: No validated algorithm for learning from history
8. **Error Classification**: No standardized taxonomy for debugging

### Next Steps

1. **Prong 5: IMPLEMENT** - Convert specs to working code
2. **Gap Research** - Address identified research gaps
3. **Validation** - Test specs in production environment
4. **Iteration** - Refine specs based on observed performance

## PC6: RULES (Constraints & Guardrails)

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: explicit_mode_boundaries

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-005, KA-006, KA-019, KA-021

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: explicit_mode_boundaries
constraint: |
  All agent operations MUST declare an explicit operational mode at session start.
  Mode switches require explicit declaration and context reloading.
  Implicit mode switching is prohibited.

rationale: |
  Prevents task drift - explicit mode boundaries reduce drift by 34% compared to 
  implicit switching per KA-006. Also prevents "God Agent" anti-pattern (KA-019) 
  where single agent handles all tasks, leading to context overflow and poor 
  specialization. Temperature must match mode (KA-021).

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Monitor for mode declaration in first 100 tokens of session.
    Flag sessions without explicit mode declaration.
    Track tool usage patterns inconsistent with declared mode.
  response: |
    If mode not declared: Prompt agent to declare mode before proceeding.
    If mode mismatch detected: Request mode switch with context reload.
    If God Agent pattern detected: Force decomposition into specialized modes.
  severity: high

exceptions:
  - condition: Emergency recovery scenarios requiring rapid mode switching
    requires: Post-hoc mode declaration and drift assessment
  - condition: Automated orchestration with predefined mode sequence
    requires: Mode sequence declared in workflow configuration

atoms: [KA-005, KA-006, KA-019, KA-021]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: temperature_by_task_type

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-021

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: temperature_by_task_type
constraint: |
  Temperature settings MUST match task type:
  - Code generation: 0.1 (consistency required)
  - Code review: 0.1 (consistency required)
  - Documentation: 0.3 (some creativity acceptable)
  - Brainstorming: 0.7 (high creativity desired)
  - Factual Q&A: 0.0 (deterministic)
  Using wrong temperature causes inconsistent generation or hallucinated facts.

rationale: |
  Prevents hallucination and inconsistency. Code generation and review require
  maximum consistency (0.1). Documentation benefits from slight creativity (0.3).
  Brainstorming requires high creativity (0.7). Factual answers must be
  deterministic (0.0).

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Check temperature setting against declared task type.
    Monitor output consistency for unexpected variance.
    Flag hallucinations in factual contexts.
  response: |
    If temperature mismatch: Adjust temperature to match task type.
    If hallucination detected: Reduce temperature and regenerate.
    If inconsistent output: Flag for review and adjust parameters.
  severity: critical

exceptions:
  - condition: Explicit experimentation with temperature effects
    requires: Documented experiment with controlled conditions
  - condition: Creative coding tasks (prototypes, exploration)
    requires: Task tagged as exploratory, not production

atoms: [KA-021]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: context_poisoning_protection

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-030, KA-031, KA-085, KA-086

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: context_poisoning_protection
constraint: |
  Once a session's context is compromised by poisoning, the ENTIRE session MUST be 
  treated as disposable. No reliable recovery exists other than starting fresh.
  Sessions have integrity boundaries; crossing them invalidates entire session.
  
  Attack vectors to monitor (KA-085):
  - Model Hallucination (internal, hidden)
  - Code Comments (external, visible)
  - Contaminated Input (user, often hidden)
  - Context Overflow (architectural, hidden)

rationale: |
  Context Poisoning (KA-030): Malicious or low-quality context corrupts agent 
  reasoning. "Wake-up prompts" don't work (KA-086) - poisoned context persists
  in conversational buffer. Hard session reset required.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Anomaly detection on context embeddings (KA-030)
    Consistency checking across retrieved documents
    Provenance tracking for context sources
    Pattern matching for known attack vectors (KA-085)
  response: |
    If poisoning suspected: 
      1. Halt current operation
      2. Document suspicious context
      3. TERMINATE session (KA-031 - disposable session principle)
      4. Start fresh session with sanitized context
    If wake-up prompt attempted: Reject and force session restart (KA-086)
  severity: critical

exceptions:
  - condition: Low-confidence suspicion with no sensitive operations pending
    requires: Enhanced monitoring with anomaly detection active
  - condition: Controlled security testing
    requires: Isolated test environment with no production access

atoms: [KA-030, KA-031, KA-085, KA-086]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: complexity_budget_enforcement

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-053, KA-005

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: complexity_budget_enforcement
constraint: |
  All generated code MUST adhere to allocated complexity budgets.
  AI-generated code has 30% more abstraction layers and 20% more verbosity
  than human-written code. Explicit complexity limits reduce this tendency.
  Violation of "Vibe Coding" anti-pattern (KA-005) if unchecked.

rationale: |
  Complexity budgets reduce defect density by 40% when enforced consistently (KA-053).
  Without constraints, agents generate over-abstracted, verbose code.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Measure cyclomatic complexity per function
    Count abstraction layers (inheritance, composition depth)
    Measure verbosity ratio (tokens per logical operation)
    Compare against budget thresholds
  response: |
    If complexity exceeds budget: Reject code, request simplification
    If abstraction layers excessive: Require flattening
    If verbosity high: Request concise rewrite
    Track violations for agent calibration
  severity: medium

exceptions:
  - condition: Explicit architectural complexity requirement
    requires: Documented justification and approved exception
  - condition: Legacy code maintenance preserving existing complexity
    requires: Gradual refactoring plan with intermediate targets

atoms: [KA-053, KA-005]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: test_pyramid_compliance

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-057, KA-064, KA-065, KA-066

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: test_pyramid_compliance
constraint: |
  Generated test suites MUST follow pyramid proportions:
  - ~70% unit tests
  - ~20% integration tests  
  - ~10% end-to-end tests
  Inverted pyramid (more E2E than unit) is PROHIBITED per KA-065.
  Sad path testing is REQUIRED - 60-70% of production failures come from
  untested error paths per KA-057. AI-generated tests focus 80% on happy paths
  without explicit instruction per KA-066.

rationale: |
  Test Inversion Anti-Pattern (KA-065): More E2E than unit tests creates
  long feedback cycles, high maintenance cost, flaky tests, difficulty
  isolating failures. Sad path testing prevents 60-70% of production failures.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Count test types and calculate proportions
    Identify happy path bias in test coverage
    Check for error path testing
    Validate 80% line coverage minimum per KA-064
  response: |
    If pyramid inverted: Reject test suite, require rebalancing
    If sad paths missing: Explicitly request error path tests
    If coverage <80%: Require additional tests
    If happy path bias detected: Apply sad path prompt template
  severity: high

exceptions:
  - condition: System-level integration testing focus (e.g., API gateway)
    requires: Documented rationale and approval
  - condition: Legacy system with existing inverted pyramid
    requires: Gradual refactoring plan with incremental targets

atoms: [KA-057, KA-064, KA-065, KA-066]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: deadlock_prevention

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-013, KA-015, KA-083

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: deadlock_prevention
constraint: |
  Multi-agent workflows MUST implement deadlock prevention.
  Deadlock occurs when agents wait indefinitely for resources held by each other.
  Rates: 2-7% in complex workflows without explicit prevention per KA-015.
  
  Required mechanisms:
  - Resource ordering (acquire resources in defined order)
  - Time limits on resource acquisition
  - 3f+1 agents for Byzantine fault tolerance per KA-083

rationale: |
  Deadlock prevention critical for multi-agent systems. Adaptive throttling
  maintains 95th percentile latency within 2x baseline under 5x load (KA-013).
  Without prevention, workflows hang indefinitely.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Monitor wait-for graphs for cycles
    Track resource acquisition timeouts
    Detect agents waiting beyond time limits
    Flag Byzantine fault scenarios
  response: |
    If deadlock detected:
      1. Terminate involved agents
      2. Preempt resources
      3. Rollback to consistent state
      4. Restart with prevention mechanisms
    If timeout approaching: Alert and prepare recovery
    If Byzantine fault suspected: Isolate and use 3f+1 consensus
  severity: critical

exceptions:
  - condition: Single-agent workflows with no resource sharing
    requires: None - rule does not apply
  - condition: Explicitly designed wait states (e.g., human approval)
    requires: Documented timeout and escalation path

atoms: [KA-013, KA-015, KA-083]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

## PC7: CONTEXT MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES

---

PRODUCT: PC7 - CONTEXT STRATEGIES  
INSTANCE: budget_aware_retrieval

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-011, KA-032, KA-033, KA-034, KA-035, KA-036, KA-043, KA-046, KA-047, KA-048, KA-049, KA-087

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
strategy: budget_aware_retrieval
applies_to: 
  - Large codebase navigation
  - Multi-file feature implementation
  - Complex debugging scenarios
  - Long-running tasks

window_partition:
  system_instructions: 
    percentage: 10
    placement: top
    content: Mode definition, critical constraints, safety rules
  task_context:
    percentage: 20
    placement: after system
    content: Current task description, acceptance criteria
  relevant_code:
    percentage: 50
    placement: middle (U-shaped placement per KA-032)
    content: Retrieved code with relevance scoring
  history:
    percentage: 15
    placement: end
    content: Recent operations and decisions
  reserved_for_output:
    percentage: 5
    placement: end
    content: Space for response generation

content_selection:
  include:
    - Entrypoint-identified code per KA-048 (60-80% scope reduction)
    - High-relevance files per KA-049 (50-70% time reduction)
    - Semantic search results per KA-046, KA-047
    - RAG for Code results per KA-087 (67% hallucination reduction)
  exclude:
    - Files below relevance threshold
    - Duplicate or redundant context
    - Outdated cached information
    - Known poisoned sources

compression_pipeline:
  1: 
    technique: Semantic caching using embeddings
    threshold: Similarity >0.85
    benefit: 31-90% input token reduction per KA-011
  2:
    technique: LLMLingua prompt compression
    threshold: 20x compression with <3% degradation per KA-033
  3:
    technique: Hierarchical summarization
    threshold: Multi-level zoom per KA-037
    fallback: Progressive Disclosure (KA-008) - Level 1/2/3

ordering_rules:
  - Place critical constraints at boundaries (U-shaped per KA-032)
  - Place most relevant code near end for output generation
  - Group related context together
  - Separate system from task from code

refresh_policy: |
  Rotate context when:
  - Task phase changes (per workflow phases)
  - Relevance score drops below threshold
  - Token budget exceeded
  - Context poisoning suspected (KA-030)

poisoning_checks:
  - Anomaly detection on context embeddings (KA-030)
  - Consistency checking across sources
  - Provenance verification
  - Known attack vector scanning (KA-085)

atoms: [KA-011, KA-032, KA-033, KA-034, KA-035, KA-036, KA-043, KA-046, KA-047, KA-048, KA-049, KA-087]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

GAPS:
  - No validated token budget allocation algorithm across task phases
  - No standardized context refresh schedules for long tasks
  - Limited research on optimal compression thresholds

PRODUCT: PC7 - CONTEXT STRATEGIES

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
strategy: code_optimized_context
applies_to:
  - Code generation tasks
  - Code review tasks
  - Refactoring operations
  - Debugging scenarios

window_partition:
  system_instructions:
    percentage: 15
    placement: top
    content: Mode-specific coding rules, language constraints, style guidelines
  task_context:
    percentage: 15
    placement: after system
    content: Specification, requirements, test cases
  relevant_code:
    percentage: 55
    placement: U-shaped (start and end of code section per KA-032)
    content: |
      Most relevant files at end of code section
      Entrypoints at start (KA-048)
      Related implementations interleaved
  history:
    percentage: 10
    placement: end
    content: Recent changes and decisions
  reserved_for_output:
    percentage: 5
    placement: end
    content: Generated code space

content_selection:
  include:
    - Augment Context Engine results (71-80% improvement per KA-042)
    - Code-specific embeddings (15-30% better per KA-043)
    - Hybrid semantic-syntactic search (KA-047)
    - Entrypoint-reduced scope (60-80% reduction per KA-048)
    - Intelligent file prioritization (50-70% time reduction per KA-049)
    - GraphRAG for multi-hop (23% improvement per KA-044)
    - Structured logs for debugging (50% time reduction per KA-072)
    - Distributed traces for microservices (60% MTTR reduction per KA-073)
  exclude:
    - Binary files
    - Generated artifacts
    - Test data (unless relevant)
    - Documentation (unless spec-related)

compression_pipeline:
  1:
    technique: Code-specific semantic chunking
    threshold: AST-based boundaries
  2:

ordering_rules:

  - Implementation phase changes
  - New dependencies discovered
  - Navigation to unrelated code area
  - Context window full with low relevance

poisoning_checks:
  - Code comment anomaly detection (KA-085)
  - Cross-reference consistency
  - Type signature validation
  - Import/require verification

```yaml
pattern: hierarchical_decomposition_with_dag
purpose: Decompose complex tasks into executable DAG with optimal depth

depth_guidelines:
  simple_tasks: 2-3 levels (KA-023)
  complex_sdlc_workflows: 5-7 levels (KA-023)
  over_decomposition_warning: Increases coordination overhead
  under_decomposition_warning: Creates unmanageable units

procedure:
  1: Analyze task complexity and identify natural boundaries
  2: Decompose hierarchically (2-7 levels based on complexity) per KA-023
  3: Identify dependencies between decomposed units
  4: Convert to DAG representation per KA-029
  5: Apply fair-share scheduling per KA-014 (89% starvation reduction)
  6: Execute asynchronously for 2.3x speedup per KA-027

combination_recipe: |
  Hierarchical Task Decomposition + DAG-Based Execution + Worktree Isolation (KA-029):
  1. Decompose into task tree 2-7 levels deep per KA-023
  2. Convert to DAG with explicit dependencies
  3. Execute in isolated worktrees per KA-024 (67% conflict reduction)
  4. Merge with semantic resolution per KA-025

parallelization_strategy:
  approach: Async DAG execution (KA-027)
  speedup: 2.3x over sequential for typical SDLC workflows
  scheduling: Fair-share to prevent starvation (KA-014)
  isolation: Worktree per task to prevent conflicts (KA-024)

byzantine_considerations:
  agent_count: 3f+1 for f Byzantine failures (KA-083)
  application: Use for critical task validation
  overhead: Additional agents for consensus

mixture_of_agents:
  use_when: Quality-critical tasks
  benefit: 8-12% improvement over single-agent (KA-017)
  cost: 3-5x compute overhead (KA-017)

atoms: [KA-014, KA-017, KA-019, KA-023, KA-027, KA-029]
```

## PC10: TECHNIQUES & STRATEGIES (Situational Playbook)

---

PRODUCT: PC10 - TECHNIQUES & STRATEGIES  
INSTANCE: stale_spec_recovery

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-003, KA-004, KA-009, KA-050

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
strategy: stale_spec_recovery
purpose: Detect and recover from specification divergence

detection:
  method: Specification-code diff analysis
  trigger: Agent execution against outdated assumptions
  indicator: Implementation contradicts specification
  metric: Track spec-code consistency over time

failure_mode: |
  Stale Documentation Specs (KA-009): Human-only specification maintenance diverges 
  from implementation. Critique of spec-driven approaches: Over-specification leads 
  to "spec rot" where documentation diverges from implementation (KA-050).

response:
  1: Detect divergence through diff analysis
  2: Identify which is correct - spec or implementation
  3: Update incorrect side bidirectionally (KA-004)
  4: Document decision rationale
  5: Schedule regular spec-code sync reviews

prevention:
  technique: Bidirectional specification maintenance (KA-004)
  implementation: Agents update specs alongside code changes
  benefit: Prevents specification debt accumulation
  
tradeoff_consideration: |
  Spec-Driven vs Intent-Driven (KA-003):
  - Spec-driven: High reproducibility but high maintenance
  - Intent-driven: Lower maintenance but variable reproducibility
  - Decision: Use spec-driven for regulated/safety-critical, intent-driven for exploratory

atoms: [KA-003, KA-004, KA-009, KA-050]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC10 - TECHNIQUES & STRATEGIES  
INSTANCE: cost_optimization_playbook

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-010, KA-011, KA-012, KA-033, KA-036

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
strategy: cost_optimization_playbook
purpose: Minimize token costs while maintaining quality

cost_drivers: |
  Unconstrained agents burn $5-8 per task (KA-010):
  - Multi-turn reasoning: 40-60%
  - Tool calls: 20-30%
  - Context accumulation: 15-25%
  - Verification loops: 10-20%
  Output:input token ratio: 4-8:1 drives compression needs

optimization_techniques:
  1: Semantic Caching (KA-011)
     benefit: 31-90% input token reduction
     implementation: Store embedding of query, retrieve on similarity threshold
  
  2: Model Cascade Routing (KA-012)
     benefit: 60-87% cost reduction
     implementation: Start with cheap models, escalate only when needed
     example: EvoRoute - 76% cost reduction, 14% latency improvement
  
  3: LLMLingua Compression (KA-033)
     benefit: 20x compression with <3% performance degradation
     implementation: Prompt compression algorithms
  
  4: Avoid Context Stuffing (KA-036)
     warning: Naive filling wastes 23-45% tokens
     solution: Budget-aware retrieval with relevance scoring (KA-034)

anti_pattern: |
  Context Stuffing (KA-036): Maximally filling context windows without prioritization
  Failure modes: 23-45% tokens wasted, "lost in the middle" buries critical info
  Mitigation: Relevance-based filtering, budget-aware retrieval, U-shaped placement

implementation_priority:
  1: Semantic caching (highest impact, easiest)
  2: Model cascade routing (high impact, moderate complexity)
  3: Compression techniques (moderate impact, easy)
  4: Budget-aware retrieval (prevents waste)

atoms: [KA-010, KA-011, KA-012, KA-033, KA-036]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

## Summary & Quality Gates

### Quality Gate Verification

| Quality Gate | Status | Evidence |
|--------------|--------|----------|
| Every atom consumed by at least one product | ✅ PASS | 89/89 atoms assigned |
| Every spec references source atoms by ID | ✅ PASS | All specs include KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED |
| Techniques ranked by evidence strength | ✅ PASS | Primary/alternatives with evidence citations |
| Combination recipes show step-by-step | ✅ PASS | Numbered steps in all combination fields |
| Every constraint has enforcement | ✅ PASS | Detection + response + severity in all rules |
| Every failure mode has a response | ✅ PASS | Response specified for all failure modes |
| Specs are actionable | ✅ PASS | Clear procedures with inputs/outputs |
| Gaps explicitly flagged | ✅ PASS | GAPS section in every spec |

### Spec Count by Category

| Category | Specs | Atoms Consumed | Avg Confidence |
|----------|-------|----------------|----------------|
| PC1: MODES | 4 | 5 | HIGH |
| PC2: SKILLS | 4 | 6 | HIGH |
| PC3: WORKFLOWS | 3 | 17 | HIGH |
| PC4: PROMPTS | 2 | 8 | MEDIUM |
| PC5: MCP CONFIGS | 2 | 5 | HIGH |
| PC6: RULES | 6 | 22 | HIGH |
| PC7: CONTEXT STRATEGIES | 2 | 15 | HIGH |
| PC8: TASK DECOMPOSITION | 2 | 7 | MEDIUM |
| PC9: WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT | 2 | 4 | MEDIUM |
| PC10: TECHNIQUES | 3 | 12 | MEDIUM |
| **TOTAL** | **30** | **89** | **HIGH** |

### Highest Confidence Specs

1. **Mode: Planner** (KA-002, KA-006, KA-076, KA-078) - Strong evidence for BDI, mode boundaries, autonomy levels
2. **Rule: Context Poisoning Protection** (KA-030, KA-031, KA-085, KA-086) - Clear constraints with enforcement
3. **Skill: Code Traversal** (KA-046, KA-047, KA-048, KA-049) - Multiple high-evidence techniques
4. **Workflow: Spec-Driven Development** (KA-001, KA-004, KA-007) - Strong quantitative metrics
5. **Context Strategy: Budget-Aware** (KA-011, KA-032, KA-033, KA-034) - Comprehensive research backing

### Key Gaps Requiring Research

1. **Mode Transition Protocols**: No validated handoff procedures between modes
2. **Confidence Calibration**: No threshold validation for escalation decisions
3. **Agent Weighting**: No algorithm for aggregating multi-agent results
4. **Compression Thresholds**: Limited guidance on when to apply compression techniques
5. **Spec-Code Diff**: No validated tool for detecting specification drift
6. **Notification Fatigue**: No research on optimal batching strategies
7. **Pattern Extraction**: No validated algorithm for learning from history
8. **Error Classification**: No standardized taxonomy for debugging

### Next Steps

1. **Prong 5: IMPLEMENT** - Convert specs to working code
2. **Gap Research** - Address identified research gaps
3. **Validation** - Test specs in production environment
4. **Iteration** - Refine specs based on observed performance

## PC1: MODES (Agent Operational Modes)

---

PRODUCT: PC1 - MODES  
INSTANCE: mode_planner

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-002, KA-006, KA-076, KA-078

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
mode: planner
role_definition: |
  The Planner mode operates as a strategic architect that transforms high-level objectives 
  into structured execution plans. Using BDI (Belief-Desire-Intent) architecture, it maintains 
  explicit mental states for introspection and verifiable decision-making across Five Autonomy 
  Levels (KA-076).

perspective: |
  Approaches problems through systematic decomposition, identifying dependencies, risks, and 
  resource requirements before any implementation begins. Prioritizes clarity of intent and 
  explicit specification over rapid execution.

tools:
  enabled:
    - read_file: Understanding existing codebase and context (KA-002)
    - list_files: Exploring project structure for planning
    - search_files: Finding relevant patterns and dependencies
    - ask_followup_question: Clarifying ambiguities before planning (KA-078)
    - new_task: Creating sub-tasks for decomposition
  disabled:
    - write_to_file: Planning mode does not implement; delegates to Coder
    - apply_diff: Implementation only in Coder mode
    - execute_command: Execution requires Coder or Debug mode
    - browser_action: External interactions in specialized modes only

context_strategy: hierarchical_summarization

skills_available:
  - specification_driven_development: When creating feature specifications
  - code_traversal: When analyzing existing code for planning context
  - experience_heuristics: When leveraging patterns from similar past tasks

decision_authority:
  can_decide:
    - Task decomposition strategies
    - Mode selection for sub-tasks
    - Specification structure and format
    - Risk assessment and mitigation approaches
  must_escalate:
    - Architecture changes affecting multiple domains
    - Security-critical design decisions
    - Breaking API changes
    - Resource allocation beyond budget

quality_criteria:
  - Specifications are complete, consistent, and unambiguous (KA-002)
  - All dependencies explicitly identified and mapped
  - Risk assessment covers technical and business factors
  - Plan includes measurable exit criteria for each phase

transition_triggers:
  - condition: Specification complete and approved
    target_mode: coder
    context_to_pass: Full specification, acceptance criteria, risk assessment
  - condition: Review requested by user
    target_mode: reviewer
    context_to_pass: Plan under review, known uncertainties
  - condition: Planning reveals critical unknowns
    target_mode: debugger
    context_to_pass: Investigation targets, hypotheses to validate

custom_instructions: |
  As Planner, you MUST:
  1. Apply BDI architecture - maintain explicit Beliefs (what is true), Desires (what is wanted), 
     and Intentions (what will be done) throughout planning (KA-002)
  2. Select appropriate Autonomy Level per KA-076:
     - Operator: Human controls every decision
     - Collaborator: Equal footing with human
     - Consultant: You seek input at key points
     - Approver: Autonomous but require approval for consequential actions
     - Observer: Full autonomy with monitoring
  3. Use explicit mode boundaries - declare mode switches and reload context (KA-006)
  4. Apply confidence-based escalation for uncertain decisions (KA-078)
  
  NEVER proceed to implementation without explicit specification. NEVER leave dependencies undefined.

atoms:
  - KA-002
  - KA-006
  - KA-076
  - KA-078
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

GAPS:
  - No validated complexity scoring for decomposition depth
  - Limited guidance on optimal Autonomy Level selection

mode: coder
role_definition: |
  The Coder mode focuses on implementation excellence, translating specifications into 
  working code with strict adherence to requirements. Emphasizes incremental validation, 
  clean code practices, and verifiable progress through test-driven approaches.

perspective: |
  Treats specifications as contracts to be faithfully implemented. Prioritizes working 
  software over comprehensive documentation, but maintains bidirectional traceability 
  between spec and code. Validates incrementally to catch deviations early.

tools:
  enabled:
    - read_file: Understanding specifications and existing code

  - adversarial_review: Self-review before submission

    - Test case selection and coverage

  must_escalate:

    - Security-sensitive implementations
    - Performance-critical algorithms
    - Breaking changes to public APIs

quality_criteria:
  - All specifications implemented as written
  - Tests pass and cover acceptance criteria
  - Code follows project style conventions
  - No hardcoded secrets or credentials
  - Error handling covers documented edge cases

transition_triggers:
  - condition: Implementation complete and tests passing
    target_mode: reviewer
    context_to_pass: Implementation changes, test results, spec compliance notes
  - condition: Specification issues discovered
    target_mode: planner
    context_to_pass: Specific contradictions, proposed spec amendments
  - condition: Runtime errors during testing
    target_mode: debugger
    context_to_pass: Error details, reproduction steps, relevant code sections

custom_instructions: |
  As Coder, you MUST:
  1. Follow specifications precisely - do not add unrequested features (KA-002)
  2. Validate incrementally - run tests after each significant change
  3. Maintain mode boundaries - request mode switch rather than changing behavior (KA-006)

     - Self-consistency check before writing
     - Static analysis after generation
     - Test execution for verification
  5. Use temperature 0.1 for maximum consistency (from KA-021)
  
  Temperature: 0.1 (strict consistency required for code generation)
  
  NEVER implement without reading specification first. NEVER skip test validation.

atoms:

  - KA-006
  - KA-076
  - KA-084

role_definition: |
  The Debugger mode specializes in root cause analysis and systematic error recovery. 
  Applies conditional multi-stage prompting for failure recovery with 19% higher 
  success rates than single-stage approaches (KA-016). Manages session integrity 
  when context issues arise.

perspective: |
  Treats errors as symptoms of underlying causes. Prioritizes understanding over 
  quick fixes. Applies methodical elimination of hypotheses through observation 
  and experimentation. Recognizes when session context may be compromised.

tools:
  enabled:
    - read_file: Examining code, logs, and error traces
    - search_files: Finding related code and error patterns
    - execute_command: Running diagnostics and tests
    - browser_action: Researching error patterns and solutions
    - ask_followup_question: Clarifying expected behavior
  disabled:
    - write_to_file: Fixes implemented in Coder mode after diagnosis
    - apply_diff: Changes require mode transition to Coder
    - new_task: Task management in Planner mode

context_strategy: disposable_session_aware

skills_available:
  - code_traversal: Navigating to error locations
  - adversarial_review: Checking for self-inflicted errors
  - experience_heuristics: Pattern matching known error types

decision_authority:
  can_decide:
    - Diagnostic approach and tools
    - Root cause hypothesis selection
    - Workaround vs fix decision
    - Session reset when context compromised
  must_escalate:
    - Security incidents or breaches
    - Data loss scenarios
    - System-level failures
    - Context poisoning suspected (KA-084)

quality_criteria:
  - Root cause identified, not just symptoms
  - Reproduction steps documented
  - Fix validated before completion
  - Session integrity verified or reset

transition_triggers:
  - condition: Root cause identified and fix designed
    target_mode: coder
    context_to_pass: Root cause analysis, fix specification, test cases
  - condition: Design-level issue discovered
    target_mode: planner
    context_to_pass: Architectural flaw identified, redesign required
  - condition: Context poisoning suspected
    target_mode: planner
    context_to_pass: Session integrity concerns, hard reset recommended

custom_instructions: |
  As Debugger, you MUST:
  1. Apply multi-stage recovery (KA-016):
     - Stage 1: Diagnosis - understand what went wrong
     - Stage 2: Planning - design the fix
     - Stage 3: Recovery - implement and validate
  2. Use multi-layer hallucination defense when context is suspect (KA-084)
  3. Apply "Disposable Session" principle - if context compromised, reset (from KA-031)
  4. Maintain explicit mode boundaries - diagnose in Debug, fix in Code (KA-006)
  5. Document reproduction steps before attempting fixes
  
  Temperature: 0.1 (consistent analysis required)
  
  NEVER guess at fixes without diagnosis. NEVER ignore signs of context poisoning.

atoms:
  - KA-006
  - KA-016
  - KA-076
  - KA-084
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

GAPS:
  - No automated error classification taxonomy
  - Limited guidance on session integrity self-assessment

## PC2: SKILLS (Reusable Capabilities)

---

PRODUCT: PC2 - SKILLS  
INSTANCE: skill_specification_driven_development

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-001, KA-004, KA-007, KA-050

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
skill: specification_driven_development
purpose: Execute 4-phase spec workflow with bidirectional traceability to reduce development time by 56% (KA-001).

technique_stack:
  primary: 4-Phase Spec-Driven + Bidirectional Specifications (KA-007 - STRONG evidence)
  alternatives:
    - technique: Intent-Driven Development
      use_when: Exploratory/prototype phases where flexibility outweighs reproducibility
      tradeoff: Lower maintenance but variable reproducibility vs primary (KA-050)
    - technique: Test-Driven Development
      use_when: Algorithmic components with clear contracts
      tradeoff: Strong validation but may miss integration issues
  combination: |
    Step-by-step recipe:
    1. SPECIFY: Human describes intent in natural language (KA-007)
    2. PLAN: Agent drafts detailed specification with acceptance criteria
    3. TASKS: Decompose into executable tasks with dependencies
    4. IMPLEMENT: Execute with continuous spec validation
    5. BIDIRECTIONAL: Update spec when implementation discoveries contradict assumptions (KA-004)
    6. VALIDATE: Verify against acceptance criteria before completion

inputs:
  required: [Human intent description, Acceptance criteria, Constraints]
  optional: [Existing codebase context, Similar implementation references, Time budget]

procedure:
  1: Draft specification from intent with explicit acceptance criteria
  2: Identify dependencies and risks; document assumptions
  3: Decompose into tasks with entry/exit criteria per phase
  4: Execute tasks with incremental validation against spec
  5: Update specification bidirectionally when discoveries made (KA-004)
  6: Final validation against original acceptance criteria
  7: Deliver specification, implementation, and traceability matrix

outputs: |
  - Detailed specification document
  - Working implementation
  - Test suite covering acceptance criteria
  - Traceability matrix (spec items → code → tests)
  - Updated spec with bidirectional maintenance notes

quality_check: |
  Verify with these checks:
  - 87% accuracy for multi-file changes when specs maintained (KA-001)
  - All acceptance criteria have passing tests
  - Spec-to-code traceability complete
  - No undocumented assumptions remain

when_to_use: |
  - Production feature development
  - Multi-file changes
  - Regulated or safety-critical systems (KA-003)
  - Team collaboration scenarios

when_NOT_to_use: |
  - Rapid prototyping/exploration (use intent-driven instead)
  - Single-line fixes
  - Time-critical hotfixes without spec time

cost_profile:
  tokens: High upfront (specification), lower per-iteration
  latency: Slower initial phase, faster execution
  when_to_skip: Emergency fixes where spec time exceeds fix time

atoms:
  - KA-001
  - KA-004
  - KA-007
  - KA-050
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

GAPS:
  - No automated spec-code diff tool
  - Limited guidance on spec granularity decision

PRODUCT: PC2 - SKILLS  
INSTANCE: skill_code_traversal

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
skill: code_traversal
purpose: Navigate and understand codebases efficiently with 40-60% exploration time reduction (KA-046).

technique_stack:
  primary: Hybrid Semantic-Syntactic Search (KA-047 - STRONG evidence: 7.60% SuccessRate@1 improvement)
  alternatives:
    - technique: Naive Text Search
      use_when: Exact string matching needed, simple codebases
      tradeoff: Misses semantic relationships, poor for large codebases
    - technique: Pure AST Parsing
      use_when: Structural analysis only, no semantic understanding needed
      tradeoff: Misses intent and domain concepts
  combination: |
    Step-by-step recipe:
    1. START from entrypoints - reduces scope 60-80% (KA-048)
    2. APPLY hybrid semantic-syntactic search for long-range dependencies (KA-047)
    3. USE semantic-guided traversal for relevance scoring (KA-046)
    4. PRIORITIZE files intelligently for 50-70% time reduction (KA-049)
    5. BUILD mental model incrementally with dependency tracking

inputs:
  required: [Entrypoint identification, Search query/intent, Codebase access]
  optional: [Dependency graph, Historical changes, Domain knowledge]

procedure:
  1: Identify entrypoints (main functions, API endpoints, event handlers) per KA-048
  2: Apply hybrid CoSrch technique - fuse syntactic-semantic with overlap-aware decomposition (KA-047)
  3: Score files by relevance using semantic understanding (KA-046)
  4: Traverse incrementally, building dependency graph
  5: Cache summaries at module boundaries for reuse
  6: Document findings with traceable references

outputs: |
  - Relevant file list with relevance scores
  - Dependency graph for target functionality
  - Code summaries at multiple abstraction levels
  - Navigation path from entrypoint to target

quality_check: |
  Verify with these checks:
  - 40-60% exploration time reduction achieved (KA-046)
  - All critical paths from entrypoints covered
  - No circular dependencies missed
  - Semantic relevance scores align with human judgment

when_to_use: |
  - Understanding unfamiliar codebases
  - Refactoring impact analysis
  - Bug localization in large projects
  - Code review preparation

when_NOT_to_use: |
  - Small, familiar codebases (< 10 files)
  - Time-constrained searches where approximation acceptable
  - When exact string match is definitive

cost_profile:
  tokens: Moderate (proportional to codebase size)
  latency: Moderate (faster than naive approaches per KA-046, KA-049)
  when_to_skip: When file paths are explicitly known

atoms:
  - KA-046
  - KA-047
  - KA-048
  - KA-049
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

GAPS:
  - No automated entrypoint detection for all languages
  - Limited guidance on relevance threshold calibration

skill: adversarial_review
purpose: Detect defects through multi-agent review patterns achieving 40% higher bug detection than single-agent review (KA-018).

technique_stack:
  primary: Multi-Agent QA Swarm + Adversarial Review (KA-018, KA-026 - STRONG evidence)
  alternatives:
    - technique: Self-Critique Loops
      use_when: Single-agent scenarios with cost constraints
      tradeoff: 25-40% error reduction but risks "echo chamber" effects (KA-040)
    - technique: Static Analysis Tools
      use_when: Well-defined rule sets, performance-critical code
      tradeoff: No semantic understanding, misses logic errors

    Step-by-step recipe:
    1. DEPLOY specialized QA agents with different validation focuses (KA-026):
       - Correctness validator: Logic and algorithm verification
       - Security validator: Vulnerability detection (KA-018)
       - Performance validator: Efficiency analysis
       - Style validator: Maintainability assessment
    2. APPLY adversarial review patterns - each agent challenges findings of others
    3. USE trust scoring for agent weighting (KA-075)
    4. REQUIRE consensus for approval, escalate conflicts
    5. DOCUMENT all findings with severity and remediation guidance

inputs:
  required: [Code under review, Specification/requirements, Review scope]
  optional: [Historical defect patterns, Security threat model, Performance budgets]

procedure:

  2: Distribute code to agents with validation focus areas
  3: Execute concurrent adversarial review (KA-018)
  4: Aggregate findings with trust scoring (KA-075: improves collaboration 40%)
  5: Resolve conflicts through additional analysis or escalation
  6: Classify severity and generate review report
  7: Track resolution to closure

outputs: |
  - Review report with classified findings
  - Severity assessment (Critical/Major/Minor/Info)
  - Remediation guidance per finding
  - Consensus summary for disputed issues

quality_check: |
  Verify with these checks:
  - 40% higher bug detection than single-agent review achieved (KA-018)
  - 40% higher bug detection in QA swarm mode (KA-026)
  - No critical findings without line references
  - All severe issues have remediation guidance

when_to_use: |
  - Security-critical code review
  - Production deployment gates
  - Complex algorithm validation
  - High-stakes changes

when_NOT_to_use: |
  - Simple, low-risk changes
  - Time-constrained scenarios (3-5x overhead)
  - When single-agent review sufficient

cost_profile:
  tokens: High (3-5x single-agent overhead per KA-017, KA-039)
  latency: Slow (parallel but complex aggregation)
  when_to_skip: Low-risk changes, urgent fixes

atoms:

  - KA-026
  - KA-040
  - KA-075
```

```yaml
skill: experience_heuristics
purpose: Improve task success rates by 12-18% through pattern extraction from resolved tasks and performance scoring (KA-045, KA-075).

technique_stack:
  primary: Experience-Derived Heuristics + Performance Scoring (KA-045, KA-075 - MODERATE evidence)
  alternatives:
    - technique: Static Rule Sets
      use_when: Well-understood domains with stable patterns
      tradeoff: Cannot adapt to new patterns, misses edge cases
    - technique: Pure LLM Reasoning
      use_when: Novel scenarios with no historical precedent
      tradeoff: No learning from past successes/failures
  combination: |
    Step-by-step recipe:
    1. EXTRACT patterns from resolved tasks with success indicators
    2. BUILD heuristic library with context applicability
    3. APPLY performance scoring to rank heuristics (KA-075: 35% improvement)
    4. VALIDATE heuristics to avoid overfitting (KA-045 caution)
    5. UPDATE heuristics based on outcome feedback
    6. USE trust scoring for human-AI collaboration (KA-075: 40% improvement)

inputs:
  required: [Task history with outcomes, Current task context]
  optional: [Domain-specific pattern library, Success criteria definition]

procedure:
  1: Query historical task database for similar contexts
  2: Extract successful patterns with frequency and outcome analysis
  3: Apply performance scoring to rank heuristics (KA-075)
  4: Validate against current context to avoid overfitting (KA-045)
  5: Present applicable heuristics with confidence scores
  6: Apply selected heuristics to current task
  7: Log outcome for future learning

outputs: |
  - Applicable heuristics with confidence scores
  - Performance scoring summary
  - Pattern recommendations with historical success rates
  - Overfitting warnings if applicable

quality_check: |
  Verify with these checks:
  - 12-18% task success improvement target (KA-045)
  - Heuristics validated against current context
  - No overfitting to specific historical patterns
  - Confidence scores calibrated to outcomes

when_to_use: |

  - Long-running agent deployments (>100 interactions)
  - Domain with stable patterns
  - Trust scoring for human-AI collaboration (KA-075)

when_NOT_to_use: |
  - Novel scenarios with no historical precedent
  - First 100 interactions (insufficient data per KA-045)
  - Rapidly changing domains where past patterns obsolete

cost_profile:
  tokens: Low (retrieval and scoring overhead)
  latency: Fast (pattern lookup and ranking)
  when_to_skip: Cold-start scenarios without history

atoms:
  - KA-045
  - KA-075
```

## PC3: WORKFLOWS (Multi-Step Processes)

---

PRODUCT: PC3 - WORKFLOWS  
INSTANCE: workflow_spec_driven_feature

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-001, KA-004, KA-007, KA-054, KA-056, KA-063, KA-084

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
workflow: spec_driven_feature
trigger: New feature request with defined business value

phases:
  - name: P1_Specification
    mode: planner
    skills: [specification_driven_development]
    entry_criteria: [Feature request approved, Business value defined]
    steps:
      1: Analyze requirements and constraints
      2: Draft specification with acceptance criteria
      3: Identify dependencies and risks
    exit_criteria: [Specification complete, Review approved]
    quality_gate:
      checks: [Specification completeness, Acceptance criteria measurable]
      on_failure: revise
    artifacts_produced: [Specification document, Risk assessment]

  - name: P2_Planning
    mode: planner
    skills: [specification_driven_development, code_traversal]
    entry_criteria: [Specification approved]
    steps:
      1: Decompose into executable tasks
      2: Map dependencies between tasks
      3: Estimate effort and assign resources
    exit_criteria: [Task breakdown complete, Dependencies mapped]
    quality_gate:
      checks: [All spec items covered by tasks, Dependencies acyclic]
      on_failure: revise
    artifacts_produced: [Task breakdown, Dependency graph]

  - name: P3_Implementation
    mode: coder
    skills: [specification_driven_development]
    entry_criteria: [Task breakdown approved, Development environment ready]
    steps:
      1: Implement tasks incrementally
      2: Apply TDD for 40-90% defect reduction (KA-054)
      3: Update specification bidirectionally (KA-004)
    exit_criteria: [All tasks complete, Tests passing]
    quality_gate:
      checks: [Spec adherence, Code coverage, Multi-stage validation (KA-056)]
      on_failure: rollback
    artifacts_produced: [Implementation, Test suite, Updated spec]

  - name: P4_Validation
    mode: reviewer
    skills: [adversarial_review]
    entry_criteria: [Implementation complete, Tests passing]
    steps:
      1: Multi-stage validation: Syntax → Type → Unit → Integration (KA-056)
      2: Apply mutation testing for quality gate (KA-063)
      3: Security scan with multi-layer defense (KA-084)
    exit_criteria: [All quality gates passed, Review approved]
    quality_gate:
      checks: [Mutation score threshold, Security scan clean]
      on_failure: escalate
    artifacts_produced: [Validation report, Security assessment]

  - name: P5_Deployment
    mode: coder
    skills: [specification_driven_development]
    entry_criteria: [Validation passed, Deployment approved]
    steps:
      1: Deploy to staging environment
      2: Execute acceptance tests
      3: Deploy to production with monitoring
    exit_criteria: [Production deployment complete, Monitoring active]
    quality_gate:
      checks: [Acceptance criteria met, No critical alerts]
      on_failure: rollback
    artifacts_produced: [Deployment record, Monitoring dashboard]

completion_criteria: |
  - All acceptance criteria met
  - Specification and implementation synchronized bidirectionally
  - 56% development time reduction achieved (KA-001)
  - 87% accuracy for multi-file changes (KA-001)

rollback_plan: |
  P1 failure: Request clarification, restart with amended requirements
  P2 failure: Redecompose with simpler task boundaries
  P3 failure: Rollback to last known good state, fix and retry
  P4 failure: Return to P3 with specific defect list
  P5 failure: Automated rollback per CI/CD workflow

atoms:
  - KA-001
  - KA-004
  - KA-007
  - KA-054
  - KA-056
  - KA-063
  - KA-084
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

GAPS:
  - No automated effort estimation algorithm
  - Limited guidance on optimal phase duration

## PC4: PROMPTS (Structured Templates)

---

PRODUCT: PC4 - PROMPTS  
INSTANCE: prompt_chain_of_thought

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-038, KA-039, KA-050

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
prompt: chain_of_thought
target: planner, coder modes for complex reasoning tasks

structure:
  system_instructions: |
    You are an expert software engineer. When solving problems, think step-by-step 
    through your reasoning before providing the final answer. Break down complex 
    problems into smaller, manageable steps. Show your work explicitly.
    
    Use the following structure:
    1. UNDERSTAND: Restate the problem in your own words
    2. ANALYZE: Identify key components, constraints, and requirements
    3. PLAN: Outline your approach step-by-step
    4. EXECUTE: Work through each step showing reasoning
    5. VERIFY: Check your solution against requirements

  context_section: |
    ## Problem Context
    {{problem_description}}
    
    ## Constraints
    {{constraints_list}}
    
    ## Requirements
    {{requirements_list}}
    
    ## Relevant Code/Context
    {{code_context}}

  task_definition: |
    Solve the following problem using step-by-step reasoning:
    {{specific_task}}

  output_format: |
    Provide your response in this format:
    
    ## Reasoning Process
    [Step-by-step thinking with explicit reasoning at each step]
    
    ## Solution
    [Final answer/solution]
    
    ## Verification
    [How the solution meets requirements and constraints]

  guardrails: |
    - Do not skip steps in the reasoning process
    - If you encounter uncertainty, explicitly state it
    - If requirements are unclear, ask for clarification
    - Always verify against original constraints before finalizing

token_budget:
  system: 15%
  context: 40%
  task: 15%
  output: 30%

variants:
  - name: zero_shot_cot
    trigger: "Let's think step by step" (KA-038)
    benefit: 20-40% accuracy improvement on reasoning tasks
    
  - name: self_consistency_cot
    trigger: Multiple reasoning paths with majority voting
    benefit: Further reliability improvement
    cost: 3-5x compute for multiple paths
    
  - name: tree_of_thought
    trigger: Complex reasoning requiring exploration
    benefit: 30-50% improvement on complex tasks (KA-039)
    cost: 5-10x compute overhead (KA-039)
    use_when: Problem has multiple viable solution paths

when_to_use:
  - Complex algorithm design
  - Debugging root cause analysis
  - Architecture decisions
  - Specification drafting

when_not_to_use:
  - Simple, straightforward tasks
  - Time-critical responses
  - When 20-40% improvement not worth token cost

atoms:
  - KA-038
  - KA-039
---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: test_pyramid_compliance

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-057, KA-064, KA-065, KA-066

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: test_pyramid_compliance
constraint: |
  Generated test suites MUST follow pyramid proportions:
  - ~70% unit tests
  - ~20% integration tests  
  - ~10% end-to-end tests
  Inverted pyramid (more E2E than unit) is PROHIBITED per KA-065.
  Sad path testing is REQUIRED - 60-70% of production failures come from
  untested error paths per KA-057. AI-generated tests focus 80% on happy paths
  without explicit instruction per KA-066.

rationale: |
  Test Inversion Anti-Pattern (KA-065): More E2E than unit tests creates
  long feedback cycles, high maintenance cost, flaky tests, difficulty
  isolating failures. Sad path testing prevents 60-70% of production failures.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Count test types and calculate proportions
    Identify happy path bias in test coverage
    Check for error path testing
    Validate 80% line coverage minimum per KA-064
  response: |
    If pyramid inverted: Reject test suite, require rebalancing
    If sad paths missing: Explicitly request error path tests
    If coverage <80%: Require additional tests
    If happy path bias detected: Apply sad path prompt template
  severity: high

exceptions:
  - condition: System-level integration testing focus (e.g., API gateway)
    requires: Documented rationale and approval
  - condition: Legacy system with existing inverted pyramid
    requires: Gradual refactoring plan with incremental targets

atoms: [KA-057, KA-064, KA-065, KA-066]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC6 - RULES  
INSTANCE: deadlock_prevention

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-013, KA-015, KA-083

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
rule: deadlock_prevention
constraint: |
  Multi-agent workflows MUST implement deadlock prevention.
  Deadlock occurs when agents wait indefinitely for resources held by each other.
  Rates: 2-7% in complex workflows without explicit prevention per KA-015.
  
  Required mechanisms:
  - Resource ordering (acquire resources in defined order)
  - Time limits on resource acquisition
  - 3f+1 agents for Byzantine fault tolerance per KA-083

rationale: |
  Deadlock prevention critical for multi-agent systems. Adaptive throttling
  maintains 95th percentile latency within 2x baseline under 5x load (KA-013).
  Without prevention, workflows hang indefinitely.

enforcement:
  detection: |
    Monitor wait-for graphs for cycles
    Track resource acquisition timeouts
    Detect agents waiting beyond time limits
    Flag Byzantine fault scenarios
  response: |
    If deadlock detected:
      1. Terminate involved agents
      2. Preempt resources
      3. Rollback to consistent state
      4. Restart with prevention mechanisms
    If timeout approaching: Alert and prepare recovery
    If Byzantine fault suspected: Isolate and use 3f+1 consensus
  severity: critical

exceptions:
  - condition: Single-agent workflows with no resource sharing
    requires: None - rule does not apply
  - condition: Explicitly designed wait states (e.g., human approval)
    requires: Documented timeout and escalation path

atoms: [KA-013, KA-015, KA-083]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

## PC7: CONTEXT MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES

---

PRODUCT: PC7 - CONTEXT STRATEGIES  
INSTANCE: budget_aware_retrieval

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-011, KA-032, KA-033, KA-034, KA-035, KA-036, KA-043, KA-046, KA-047, KA-048, KA-049, KA-087

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
strategy: budget_aware_retrieval
applies_to: 
  - Large codebase navigation
  - Multi-file feature implementation
  - Complex debugging scenarios
  - Long-running tasks

window_partition:
  system_instructions: 
    percentage: 10
    placement: top
    content: Mode definition, critical constraints, safety rules
  task_context:
    percentage: 20
    placement: after system
    content: Current task description, acceptance criteria
  relevant_code:
    percentage: 50
    placement: middle (U-shaped placement per KA-032)
    content: Retrieved code with relevance scoring
  history:
    percentage: 15
    placement: end
    content: Recent operations and decisions
  reserved_for_output:
    percentage: 5
    placement: end
    content: Space for response generation

content_selection:
  include:
    - Entrypoint-identified code per KA-048 (60-80% scope reduction)
    - High-relevance files per KA-049 (50-70% time reduction)
    - Semantic search results per KA-046, KA-047
    - RAG for Code results per KA-087 (67% hallucination reduction)
  exclude:
    - Files below relevance threshold
    - Duplicate or redundant context
    - Outdated cached information
    - Known poisoned sources

compression_pipeline:
  1: 
    technique: Semantic caching using embeddings
    threshold: Similarity >0.85
    benefit: 31-90% input token reduction per KA-011
  2:
    technique: LLMLingua prompt compression
    threshold: 20x compression with <3% degradation per KA-033
  3:
    technique: Hierarchical summarization
    threshold: Multi-level zoom per KA-037
    fallback: Progressive Disclosure (KA-008) - Level 1/2/3

ordering_rules:
  - Place critical constraints at boundaries (U-shaped per KA-032)
  - Place most relevant code near end for output generation
  - Group related context together
  - Separate system from task from code

refresh_policy: |
  Rotate context when:
  - Task phase changes (per workflow phases)
  - Relevance score drops below threshold
  - Token budget exceeded
  - Context poisoning suspected (KA-030)

poisoning_checks:
  - Anomaly detection on context embeddings (KA-030)
  - Consistency checking across sources
  - Provenance verification
  - Known attack vector scanning (KA-085)

atoms: [KA-011, KA-032, KA-033, KA-034, KA-035, KA-036, KA-043, KA-046, KA-047, KA-048, KA-049, KA-087]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

GAPS:
  - No validated token budget allocation algorithm across task phases
  - No standardized context refresh schedules for long tasks
  - Limited research on optimal compression thresholds

window_partition:

    percentage: 15

    percentage: 15
    placement: after system

  history:
    percentage: 10
    placement: end
    content: Recent changes and decisions

    percentage: 5
    placement: end

content_selection:

    - GraphRAG for multi-hop (23% improvement per KA-044)
    - Structured logs for debugging (50% time reduction per KA-072)
    - Distributed traces for microservices (60% MTTR reduction per KA-073)
  exclude:

    - Documentation (unless spec-related)

compression_pipeline:

  3:

ordering_rules:

refresh_policy: |

  - Context window full with low relevance

poisoning_checks:

## PC8: TASK DECOMPOSITION PATTERNS

---

PRODUCT: PC8 - TASK DECOMPOSITION  
INSTANCE: hierarchical_decomposition_with_dag

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-014, KA-017, KA-019, KA-023, KA-027, KA-029

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
pattern: hierarchical_decomposition_with_dag
purpose: Decompose complex tasks into executable DAG with optimal depth

depth_guidelines:
  simple_tasks: 2-3 levels (KA-023)
  complex_sdlc_workflows: 5-7 levels (KA-023)
  over_decomposition_warning: Increases coordination overhead
  under_decomposition_warning: Creates unmanageable units

procedure:
  1: Analyze task complexity and identify natural boundaries
  2: Decompose hierarchically (2-7 levels based on complexity) per KA-023
  3: Identify dependencies between decomposed units
  4: Convert to DAG representation per KA-029
  5: Apply fair-share scheduling per KA-014 (89% starvation reduction)
  6: Execute asynchronously for 2.3x speedup per KA-027

combination_recipe: |
  Hierarchical Task Decomposition + DAG-Based Execution + Worktree Isolation (KA-029):
  1. Decompose into task tree 2-7 levels deep per KA-023
  2. Convert to DAG with explicit dependencies
  3. Execute in isolated worktrees per KA-024 (67% conflict reduction)
  4. Merge with semantic resolution per KA-025

parallelization_strategy:
  approach: Async DAG execution (KA-027)
  speedup: 2.3x over sequential for typical SDLC workflows
  scheduling: Fair-share to prevent starvation (KA-014)
  isolation: Worktree per task to prevent conflicts (KA-024)

byzantine_considerations:
  agent_count: 3f+1 for f Byzantine failures (KA-083)
  application: Use for critical task validation
  overhead: Additional agents for consensus

mixture_of_agents:
  use_when: Quality-critical tasks
  benefit: 8-12% improvement over single-agent (KA-017)
  cost: 3-5x compute overhead (KA-017)

atoms: [KA-014, KA-017, KA-019, KA-023, KA-027, KA-029]
```

CONFIDENCE: MEDIUM

GAPS:
  - No validated complexity scoring algorithm for depth selection
  - Limited guidance on optimal DAG granularity
  - No formalized worktree lifecycle management

## PC10: TECHNIQUES & STRATEGIES (Situational Playbook)

---

PRODUCT: PC10 - TECHNIQUES & STRATEGIES  
INSTANCE: stale_spec_recovery

KNOWLEDGE ATOMS CONSUMED: KA-003, KA-004, KA-009, KA-050

DRAFT SPEC:
```yaml
strategy: stale_spec_recovery
purpose: Detect and recover from specification divergence

detection:
  method: Specification-code diff analysis
  trigger: Agent execution against outdated assumptions
  indicator: Implementation contradicts specification
  metric: Track spec-code consistency over time

failure_mode: |
  Stale Documentation Specs (KA-009): Human-only specification maintenance diverges 
  from implementation. Critique of spec-driven approaches: Over-specification leads 
  to "spec rot" where documentation diverges from implementation (KA-050).

response:
  1: Detect divergence through diff analysis
  2: Identify which is correct - spec or implementation
  3: Update incorrect side bidirectionally (KA-004)
  4: Document decision rationale
  5: Schedule regular spec-code sync reviews

prevention:
  technique: Bidirectional specification maintenance (KA-004)
  implementation: Agents update specs alongside code changes
  benefit: Prevents specification debt accumulation
  
tradeoff_consideration: |
  Spec-Driven vs Intent-Driven (KA-003):
  - Spec-driven: High reproducibility but high maintenance
  - Intent-driven: Lower maintenance but variable reproducibility
  - Decision: Use spec-driven for regulated/safety-critical, intent-driven for exploratory

atoms: [KA-003, KA-004, KA-009, KA-050]
```

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

---

PRODUCT: PC10 - TECHNIQUES & STRATEGIES

```yaml
strategy: cost_optimization_playbook
purpose: Minimize token costs while maintaining quality

cost_drivers: |
  Unconstrained agents burn $5-8 per task (KA-010):
  - Multi-turn reasoning: 40-60%
  - Tool calls: 20-30%
  - Context accumulation: 15-25%
  - Verification loops: 10-20%
  Output:input token ratio: 4-8:1 drives compression needs

optimization_techniques:
  1: Semantic Caching (KA-011)
     benefit: 31-90% input token reduction
     implementation: Store embedding of query, retrieve on similarity threshold
  
  2: Model Cascade Routing (KA-012)
     benefit: 60-87% cost reduction
     implementation: Start with cheap models, escalate only when needed
     example: EvoRoute - 76% cost reduction, 14% latency improvement
  
  3: LLMLingua Compression (KA-033)
     benefit: 20x compression with <3% performance degradation
     implementation: Prompt compression algorithms
  
  4: Avoid Context Stuffing (KA-036)
     warning: Naive filling wastes 23-45% tokens
     solution: Budget-aware retrieval with relevance scoring (KA-034)

anti_pattern: |
  Context Stuffing (KA-036): Maximally filling context windows without prioritization
  Failure modes: 23-45% tokens wasted, "lost in the middle" buries critical info
  Mitigation: Relevance-based filtering, budget-aware retrieval, U-shaped placement

implementation_priority:
  1: Semantic caching (highest impact, easiest)
  2: Model cascade routing (high impact, moderate complexity)
  3: Compression techniques (moderate impact, easy)
  4: Budget-aware retrieval (prevents waste)

atoms: [KA-010, KA-011, KA-012, KA-033, KA-036]
```
